vulnerabilities.title: Config Key Mismatch in _reconfigure Loads Wrong Values for Critical Parameters
vulnerabilities.summary: _reconfigure loads MIN_TAX_RATE from StorageKey.LockManager, MAX_TAX_RATE from StorageKey.AccountManager, DEFAULT_TAX_RATE from StorageKey.ClaimManager, BASE_SCHNIBBLE_RATE from StorageKey.MigrationManager, and PRICE_PER_PLOT from StorageKey.NFTOverlord. These StorageKeys are designed for contract addresses, not numeric configuration values. Depending on ConfigStorage implementation, getUint may return 0 or an address cast as uint256, corrupting all economic parameters.

description.desc: getUint(StorageKey.LockManager) for MIN_TAX_RATE - StorageKey intended for address resolution

vulnerabilities.impact: CRITICAL. Complete Denial of Service (DoS) of the contract. All staked NFTs become permanently locked, and the contract's economic parameters are entirely corrupted.

vulnerabilities.title: transferToUnoccupiedPlot Missing plotId Update Permanently Bricks Landlord Plots
vulnerabilities.summary: transferToUnoccupiedPlot updates plotOccupied for old and new plots but never updates toilerState[tokenId].plotId. When the renter subsequently unstakes, unstakeMunchable reads the stale plotId from toilerState and clears the OLD plot (already empty), leaving the NEW plot permanently marked as occupied with no way to clear it. Any renter can grief any landlord by calling transferToUnoccupiedPlot followed by unstakeMunchable, permanently bricking one plot per operation.

description.desc: plotOccupied[landlord][oldPlotId] and plotOccupied[landlord][plotId] are updated but toilerState[tokenId].plotId is never set to the new plotId

vulnerabilities.impact: CRITICAL. This vulnerability allows any user to permanently destroy the utility of any landlord's plots at minimal cost (gas only). It also causes state corruption that can affect other users' stakes.

vulnerabilities.title: Timestamp Underflow in Dirty Flag Path Permanently Locks NFTs in Contract
vulnerabilities.summary: When a landlord's plot count decreases (by unlocking staked value), _farmPlots uses plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated as the reward timestamp. If lastUpdated is older than toilerState[tokenId].lastToilDate (renter farmed more recently than the landlord's last metadata update), the subtraction (timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate) underflows in Solidity 0.8.25, reverting the transaction. Since unstakeMunchable, transferToUnoccupiedPlot, and farmPlots all trigger forceFarmPlots, the renter's NFT bec

description.desc: timestamp = plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated could be < _toiler.lastToilDate; then line 253 (timestamp - _toiler.lastToilDate) underflows and reverts

vulnerabilities.impact: CRITICAL. Users' staked NFTs can be permanently locked in the contract with no recovery mechanism. A malicious landlord could intentionally trap high-value NFTs by manipulating their locked value and metadata updates.

vulnerabilities.title: Negative Bonus Int256-to-Uint256 Cast Mints Near-Infinite Schnibbles
vulnerabilities.summary: The reward formula casts a potentially negative int256 result to uint256 without checking for negativity. Due to the incorrect formula structure S*(1+B)/100 instead of S*(100+B)/100, any finalBonus <= -2 produces a negative intermediate result. Casting this to uint256 wraps to approximately 2^256, granting the attacker near-infinite schnibbles. REALM_BONUSES are int16[] designed to include negative values for cross-realm penalties, making finalBonus <= -2 highly plausible with normal configurati

description.desc: schnibblesTotal = uint256((int256(schnibblesTotal) + (int256(schnibblesTotal) * finalBonus)) / 100) — negative result from finalBonus <= -2 wraps to ~2^256 on uint256 cast

vulnerabilities.impact: CRITICAL. Depending on the landlord's tax rate, this either allows infinite minting of the protocol's primary reward currency (destroying the economy) or permanently locks user NFTs in the contract due to persistent reverts in the unstake path.

vulnerabilities.title: Off-by-One in Dirty Flag Check Allows Farming on Non-Existent Plots
vulnerabilities.summary: The dirty flag check uses _getNumPlots(landlord) < _toiler.plotId instead of <=. Since plotIds are 0-indexed and _getNumPlots returns the count, a toiler on plotId N remains valid when the landlord reduces to exactly N plots (valid range 0..N-1). The boundary plotId continues farming indefinitely without the dirty flag being set, accruing rewards on a plot that no longer exists.

description.desc: if (_getNumPlots(landlord) < _toiler.plotId) should be <= to catch boundary case where numPlots equals plotId

vulnerabilities.impact: The protocol suffers from unbacked reward inflation. Renters on boundary plots earn continuous yield that is not backed by the landlord's locked value. Additionally, the landlord is forced to pay tax on these phantom schnibbles, distorting the game's economy.

vulnerabilities.title: Dirty Flag Uses Latest Metadata Timestamp Instead of Plot Invalidation Time
vulnerabilities.summary: When the dirty flag triggers, _farmPlots uses plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated as the reward calculation timestamp. If the landlord's metadata was updated (for any reason) after the plot became invalid, lastUpdated reflects the LATEST update—not when the plot first became invalid. This grants the renter excess rewards for the period between plot invalidation and the latest metadata update.

description.desc: timestamp = plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated — this may reflect a metadata update far after the plot became invalid, granting excess reward duration

vulnerabilities.impact: This defect causes inflation of the Schnibbles reward token and unfairly distributes rewards to renters occupying invalid plots. While the protocol developers noted this edge case in comments, the economic impact scales with the time gap between plot invalidation and subsequent metadata updates, potentially leading to significant unbacked token emission.

vulnerabilities.title: Dirty-Flagged Tokens Stuck in Zero-Yield Dead State with No Recovery
vulnerabilities.summary: Once toilerState[tokenId].dirty is set to true, the token is skipped in _farmPlots (earning zero rewards) but remains staked in the contract. There is no mechanism to clear the dirty flag or transfer a dirty token to a valid plot. The renter must manually unstake and re-stake, losing all yield for the period between dirty flag activation and their noticing the condition. A landlord can strategically trigger dirty flags by reducing/re-adding locked value.

description.desc: if (_toiler.dirty) continue; — dirty tokens earn zero forever with no clearing mechanism

vulnerabilities.impact: Renters suffer a complete loss of yield from the moment the dirty flag is triggered until they manually unstake and re-stake. The intended recovery mechanism (`transferToUnoccupiedPlot`) is ineffective. This creates a griefing vector for landlords and a poor user experience.

vulnerabilities.title: Staking Limit Off-by-One Allows 11 NFTs Instead of 10
vulnerabilities.summary: The staking limit check uses `munchablesStaked[mainAccount].length > 10` instead of `>= 10`. When the array length is exactly 10 (indices 0-9 occupied), the check passes (10 > 10 = false), allowing one more push to reach 11 staked munchables. This exceeds the intended maximum of 10.

description.desc: if (munchablesStaked[mainAccount].length > 10) should be >= 10 to enforce max of 10

vulnerabilities.impact: Users can bypass the intended staking limit of 10 NFTs per account, gaining an extra slot (11 total). This provides a ~10% advantage in yield generation capacity over the intended limit.

vulnerabilities.title: Any registered user can grief their own staked NFT into an unrecoverable state if farming math reverts, because unstake is force-coupled to farm
vulnerabilities.summary: unstakeMunchable is guarded by forceFarmPlots(msg.sender), which executes _farmPlots before allowing unstake. If any single staked token causes _farmPlots to revert (due to config mistakes, bonus underflow, tax underflow, or dependency revert), the user cannot unstake any of their tokens, locking all custodied NFTs for that account. Attackers can deliberately create such a condition for themselves (or get victims into it via landlord-side settings if those are unconstrained).

description.desc: modifier forceFarmPlots(_account){ _farmPlots(_account); _; } combined with unstakeMunchable(... ) forceFarmPlots(msg.sender); _farmPlots contains unchecked arithmetic and external calls that can revert

vulnerabilities.impact: High. Custodied user assets (NFTs) can become permanently locked in the contract due to operational errors, malicious landlord actions, or edge cases in the reward math. This violates the core security property that users should always be able to exit the system.

vulnerabilities.title: Registration gating can cause unexpected permanent NFT lock if AccountManager registration is revoked/changed after staking
vulnerabilities.summary: All user entry points (stake/unstake/transfer/farm) call _getMainAccountRequireRegistered which reverts if Player.registrationDate == 0. If AccountManager has any mechanism that can reset registrationDate to 0 (e.g., migration, slashing, admin correction, bug), a previously staked user may become unable to call farm/unstake/transfer, leaving NFTs custodied indefinitely.
vulnerabilities.impact: High impact (permanent loss of access to staked NFTs) but dependent on external operational factors (AccountManager state changes or upgrades).

vulnerabilities.title: Schnibbles Formula Divides Entire Sum by 100 Instead of Only Bonus — 100x Reward Reduction
vulnerabilities.summary: The reward formula computes `(schnibblesTotal + schnibblesTotal * finalBonus) / 100` which equals `schnibblesTotal * (1 + finalBonus) / 100`. With finalBonus=0 (no bonus), users receive schnibblesTotal/100 instead of schnibblesTotal — a 100x reduction. The correct formula should be `schnibblesTotal + (schnibblesTotal * finalBonus / 100)` = `schnibblesTotal * (100 + finalBonus) / 100`.

description.desc: schnibblesTotal = uint256((int256(schnibblesTotal) + (int256(schnibblesTotal) * finalBonus)) / 100); divides the entire expression by 100 instead of only the bonus portion

vulnerabilities.impact: CRITICAL. The core economic mechanism of the protocol (farming Schnibbles) is fundamentally broken. 99% of intended rewards are lost, and the bonus system behaves erratically (e.g., a 100% bonus is required just to receive the base rate).

vulnerabilities.title: Misconfigured Tax Rate Bounds Allow >100% Tax Rate Causing Renter Schnibbles Underflow and NFT Lock
vulnerabilities.summary: MAX_TAX_RATE is loaded from StorageKey.AccountManager via getUint, which may return an address-derived value or incorrect number. If MAX_TAX_RATE > 1e18, a landlord can call updateTaxRate with a value exceeding 100% (1e18). In _farmPlots, schnibblesLandlord = (schnibblesTotal * latestTaxRate) / 1e18 would exceed schnibblesTotal. The subsequent `renterMetadata.unfedSchnibbles += (schnibblesTotal - schnibblesLandlord)` underflows, reverting _farmPlots and locking the renter's NFT via forceFarmPlot

description.desc: MAX_TAX_RATE loaded from address-type key; updateTaxRate validates against this potentially oversized bound; _farmPlots subtracts schnibblesLandlord from schnibblesTotal without checking schnibblesLandlord <= schnibblesTotal

vulnerabilities.impact: High. Renters lose their staked NFTs permanently due to the inability to unstake, resulting in a complete loss of assets for affected users.

vulnerabilities.title: Landlord Tax Rate Bait-and-Switch Exploits Next-Period Rate Lock
vulnerabilities.summary: updateTaxRate does not trigger farming for existing stakers before changing the rate. While latestTaxRate in toilerState protects the current period, the NEW rate is immediately stored for the NEXT period when any staker farms. A landlord can attract stakers with a low tax rate, wait for them to farm (locking the low rate for the current period), then immediately raise the rate to MAX. All subsequent farming periods use MAX tax rate. The renters must notice and unstake manually, losing time-valu

description.desc: updateTaxRate directly sets plotMetadata[landlord].currentTaxRate without farming existing stakers; _farmPlots line 262 stores new rate as latestTaxRate for next period

vulnerabilities.impact: None. The system functions as intended and protects users from mid-period rate changes.
